data.title: Otis buys majority stake in WeMaintain to bolster tech-driven service
data.description:
Otis Worldwide Corp. (OTIS) on Monday said it acquired a majority stake in WeMaintain, a technology-enabled elevator and escalator service company, the firms said Monday. Financial terms of the agreement weren’t disclosed.

The deal underscores Otis’ (OTIS) push to modernize its service business with digital tools, as customers increasingly demand real-time performance visibility and improved reliability across vertical transportation systems.

“Service is the foundation of our business, and innovation in how service is delivered is increasingly important,” Judy Marks, chair, chief executive and president of Otis (OTIS), said in a statement. She pointed to WeMaintain’s technology platform and agile operating model as aligned with the evolving needs of a more digital, customer-centric environment.

WeMaintain will continue to operate independently, maintaining its existing customer relationships and offering its agnostic Internet of Things and artificial intelligence-based solutions across the market.

Chief executive Benoit Dupont said the investment allows the company to scale while preserving its core focus on technology and service quality.

“With the stability and support of the global industry leader, we are well positioned to strengthen our offering,” he said.

The transaction reflects a broader trend across industrial sectors, where legacy manufacturers are investing in software and data-driven capabilities to differentiate service offerings and capture recurring revenue streams.

data.title: Halliburton wins multibillion-dollar contract from YPF for Vaca Muerta work
data.description:
Halliburton (HAL) up 1.8% pre-market Monday after saying it was awarded an exclusive multibillion-dollar contract by YPF (YPF) to provide bundled unconventional completions services in the Vaca Muerta shale play offshore Argentina.

Under the contract, Halliburton (HAL) said it will utilize its Zeus electric fracturing services in its first international deployment and include its Octiv Auto Frac service, part of its Octiv digital fracturing services operating environment that delivers consistent onsite execution while pumping.

Halliburton (HAL) said the integrated approach establishes a new benchmark for international unconventional fracturing, combining electrification, automation, and advanced digital workflows to improve efficiency, consistency, and emissions-intensity reduction efforts.

"This award significantly increases our footprint in Argentina and reflects our customers' confidence in Halliburton to deliver large-scale unconventional fracturing through technology leadership and operational excellence," the company's Western Hemisphere president Casey Maxwell said.

The Vaca Muerta shale play in Argentina's Neuquén Basin holds the world's second-largest shale gas and fourth-largest shale oil reserves.

data.title: 5N Plus appoints Alban Fournier as CFO
data.description:
• 5N Plus (VNP:CA) announced on Monday the appointment of Alban Fournier as chief financial officer, effective April 27, 2026.
  • The company said the appointment follows the company's previously announced leadership succession plan under which Richard Perron, current CFO, will become president and chief executive officer and Gervais Jacques, current CEO, will become executive chair of the Board effective May 31, 2026.
  • Fournier most recently served as chief financial and technology officer at the Montreal Port Authority, where he also oversaw procurement. Previously, he was CFO at Chantier Davie Canada. Before that, he spent more than two decades with Rio Tinto, where he held several senior leadership roles, including group head of internal audit and CFO of Rio Tinto Bauxite & Alumina.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Kering falls after Morgan Stanley warns of limited upside
data.description:
Kering SA (PPRUF) (PPRUY) fell in European trading after Morgan Stanley downgraded the global luxury stock to an Equal-weight rating after having it set at Overweight.

The firm believes that Kering's (PPRUF) (PPRUY) turnaround remains on track but sees the upside as already largely priced into the share price.  

"On our updated estimates, the stock is currently trading on a 2028e P/E of ~17x, and with most of the low-hanging fruit already picked (real estate disposal, cosmetics agreement, and balance sheet fixing), we see relatively limited upside for the shares for now," highlighted analyst Edouard Aubin.

Looking ahead, Aubin and his team said a continuation of the revamp of the organization, as well as greater conviction that Gucci's turnaround has legs, could be key catalysts in turning the firm positive on the stock again.

Kering SA (PPRUF) (PPRUY) manages a renowned portfolio of creative fashion, leather goods, jewelry, and eyewear houses, including Gucci, Saint Laurent, and Bottega Veneta.

Shares of Kering (PPRUF) (PPRUY) slid 3.3% in Paris trading on Monday.

data.title: Goldman in charts: Q1 revenues from Americas rise, EMEA slows, Asia jumps; provisions for losses increase
CoTec receives $19.9M from warrant acceleration
data.description:
• CoTec (CTH:CA) announced on Monday that it has received gross proceeds of approximately $19.9M from the exercise of 16.6M warrants pursuant to its warrant acceleration announced on March 4, 2026.
  • The warrant exercise represents 95.6% of the 17.3M warrants subject to the warrant acceleration. The unexercised warrants expired on Friday, April 10, 2026.
  • The company issued 16.6M common shares pursuant to the warrant exercise, increasing the total common shares outstanding to 115.14M.
  • Julian Treger, CoTec CEO, commented, "We are very pleased with the successful outcome of the warrant acceleration during a challenging time in the markets. This is testimony to the continued support of our shareholders and recognition of the compelling value proposition of our Company."
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Perella Weinberg to expand in UK with Gleacher Shacklock acquisition
data.description:
Perella Weinberg Partners (PWP) is set to acquire the London-headquartered independent advisory firm Gleacher Shacklock.

Terms of the acquisition were not disclosed.

The transaction is expected to close in the second half.

The acquisition is expected to expand Perella Weinberg's presence in the UK market. The country is said to be the largest advisory market in Europe, and cross-border transaction volume between the UK, Europe, and North America continues to accelerate.

Shares were 1.57% higher at $20.75 during Monday pre-market trading.

data.title: Goldman Sachs GAAP EPS of $17.55 beats by $1.16, revenue of $17.23B beats by $300M
data.description:
• Goldman Sachs press release (GS): Q1 GAAP EPS of $17.55 beats by $1.16.
  • Revenue of $17.23B (+14.4% Y/Y) beats by $300M.
  • Net revenues were $17.23 billion for the first quarter of 2026, 14% higher than thefirst quarter of 2025 and 28% higher than the fourth quarter of 2025. The increasecompared with the first quarter of 2025 primarily reflected higher net revenues inGlobal Banking & Markets.
  • Annualized return on average common shareholders' equity (ROE) was 19.8% for the first quarter of 2026.
  • Book value per common share increased by 1.0% during the quarter to $361.19.
  •

data.title: ImageneBio stock jumps on $30M financing
data.description:
• ImageneBio (IMA) has entered into a definitive securities purchase agreement with certain institutional investors in a private placement that is expected to result in gross proceeds of around $30M.
  • Pursuant to the terms of the securities purchase agreement, Imagene is selling pre-funded warrants to purchase up to 5,770,335 shares at a price of $5.199 per pre-funded warrant. The pre-funded warrants will have an exercise price of $0.001 per share.
  • The financing is being led by a new investor, Coastlands Capital, and includes participation from additional new investors, including Trails Edge Capital Partners, and existing investors, including Omega Funds and OrbiMed.
  • The company plans to use the proceeds from the offering to support further development of IMG-007 in alopecia areata and late-stage development activities in atopic dermatitis.

data.title: GFL Environmental confirms acquisition of Secure Waste in ~C$6.4B deal
data.description:
GFL Environmental (GFL) down 3.6% pre-market Monday after saying it agreed to acquire Secure Waste Infrastructure (SECYF) for $24.75/common share, representing an enterprise value of ~C$6.4B through a combination of 80% in GFL subordinate voting shares and 20% in cash.

The $24.75/share purchase price represents a premium of 23% to the volume weighted average price of the Secure (SECYF) common shares for the 60 trading days ending April 10.

Secure (SECYF) operates a diversified waste management platform in western Canada and North Dakota through a network of assets across over 80 locations, including 12 landfills, 55 waste treatment facilities, 12 recycling facilities, 98 injection wells, and five transfer stations.

The acquisition "will provide us with a highly complementary network of permitted waste processing and disposal assets that will densify our footprint in western Canada, significantly enhance our scale, and expand our ability to offer customers a full suite of waste management services," GFL Environmental (GFL) Founder and CEO Patrick Dovigi said.

Two weeks ago, GFL (GFL) announced the acquisition of Frontier Waste Solutions, a network of solid waste assets across 24 sites in Texas.
